taskInput.placeholder: Add task — Enter to create, Shift+Enter for details

taskSection.emptyToday: Move tasks from To Do to start your day

taskSheet.titlePlaceholder: What do you need to do?

blockedDialog.title: Move from Blocked to {target}
blockedDialog.label: Add a note explaining the resolution

yesterday.message: You have {count} incomplete task(s) from yesterday

intentions.title: Week intentions (3–5)
intentions.hint: Enter to add · Ctrl+Enter to save

backlog.title: Backlog
backlog.empty: No tasks in backlog. Use the day view with Shift+Enter to create tasks without a day.

migration.prompt: Local tasks found. Import to your account?

help.ritual1Desc: Open the week view and define 3–5 intentions for the week. These guide your daily focus.
help.ritual2Desc: Review today's tasks. Move to TODAY only the ones you'll actually do (max 5). The rest stays in TODO.
help.ritual3Desc: Review the week view: intentions met, blocked tasks, and pending backlog. Adjust for next week.
help.stateTodoDesc: Pending task. Can be moved to TODAY or BLOCKED.
help.stateTodayDesc: Committed for today. Can be completed (DONE), moved back to TODO, or blocked.
help.stateBlockedDesc: Blocked by external dependency. Unblocking requires an explanatory note.
help.stateDoneDesc: Completed. Immutable state — cannot be reopened.
help.limitDesc: If you have more than 5 tasks in TODAY, a red warning badge appears. More than 5 real commitments in a day is unrealistic — the limit forces you to prioritize.
help.faq1Q: What happens to yesterday's incomplete tasks?
help.faq1A: They stay on their day. Today you'll see a prompt to move them.
help.faq2Q: Can I create tasks without a day?
help.faq2A: Yes, they go to the weekly backlog.
help.faq3Q: Why the limit of 5 in TODAY?
help.faq3A: More than 5 real commitments in a day is unrealistic. The limit forces prioritization.
help.faq4Q: Can I reopen a DONE task?
help.faq4A: No. If there's a follow-up, create a new task.
help.faq5A: Your data is stored in the cloud with Supabase. You can access it from any device.
